Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:README.md (unknown).

Cortex is a local knowledge substrate for AI coding agents that persists observations, decisions, and discoveries as an append-only datom log and projects them into a graph and vector store. It enables AI agents to recall, reflect on, and build upon prior work across sessions. The system operates through a write pipeline that validates, seals, and appends entries to a durable log before applying them to Neo4j (graph store) and Weaviate (vector store). Key features include episodic capture via `cortex observe`, retrieval using HippoRAG and ACT-R path-based algorithms, trail creation for grouping related work sessions, and self-healing through log replay. The architecture enforces data integrity with the datom log as the authoritative source of truth, and supports time-travel queries via historical transaction IDs. Configuration controls retrieval parameters like Personalized PageRank damping (0.85), seed top-k (10), activation weights, and visibility thresholds (0.05), while managing Ollama model usage with concurrency limits (2) and context size (32768).

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:README.md (unknown).

Cortex is a local knowledge substrate for AI coding agents that persists observations, decisions, and discoveries as an append-only datom log and projects them into a graph and vector store. It enables AI agents to recall, reflect on, and build upon prior work across sessions. The system operates through a write pipeline that validates, seals, and appends entries to a durable log before applying them to Neo4j (graph store) and Weaviate (vector store). Key features include episodic capture via `cortex observe`, retrieval using HippoRAG and ACT-R path-based algorithms, trail creation for grouping related work sessions, and self-healing through log replay. The architecture enforces data integrity with the datom log as the authoritative source of truth, and supports time-travel queries via historical transaction IDs. Configuration controls retrieval parameters like Personalized PageRank damping (0.85), seed top-k (10), activation weights, and visibility thresholds (0.05), while managing Ollama model usage with concurrency limits (2) and context size (32768).

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:internal/walker/CLAUDE.md (unknown).

Module go:per-package:internal/datom (go).

This module defines the core data structure and serialization logic for a single immutable fact in an append-only log system. The Datom struct represents a fact with fields for entity, attribute, value, transaction, timestamp, actor, operation, source, and invocation ID, with a SHA-256 checksum computed over a canonical JSON serialization of its fields. The module ensures data integrity through cryptographic checksums that detect any tampering, with a deterministic serialization order that guarantees stability across field assignment order. The checksum is computed and stored during sealing, and verified during unmarshaling. A registry tracks attribute metadata, including whether they use last-write-wins (LWW) semantics, which affects how values are replayed during log reconstruction. This design supports immutable, verifiable, and reproducible log entries with provenance and resilience against data corruption.

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:README.md (unknown).

Cortex is a local knowledge substrate for AI coding agents that persists observations, decisions, and discoveries as an append-only datom log and projects them into a graph and vector store. It enables AI agents to recall, reflect on, and build upon prior work across sessions. The system operates through a write pipeline that validates, seals, and appends entries to a durable log before applying them to Neo4j (graph store) and Weaviate (vector store). Key features include episodic capture via `cortex observe`, retrieval using HippoRAG and ACT-R path-based algorithms, trail creation for grouping related work sessions, and self-healing through log replay. The architecture enforces data integrity with the datom log as the authoritative source of truth, and supports time-travel queries via historical transaction IDs. Configuration controls retrieval parameters like Personalized PageRank damping (0.85), seed top-k (10), activation weights, and visibility thresholds (0.05), while managing Ollama model usage with concurrency limits (2) and context size (32768).

This module defines a structured set of evaluation questions for a system called Cortex, which appears to be a knowledge graph and reasoning engine. The questions cover core concepts such as datoms, activation models, retrieval pipelines, storage backends, and internal component behaviors. It explicitly references technical elements like Personalized PageRank (PPR), community detection algorithms (Leiden, Louvain), activation decay, and data structures such as ULID identifiers. The module organizes questions by ID and keyword tags, enabling users to explore specific domains—such as ingestion, recall, reflection, and model embedding—within the system. These questions serve as a diagnostic and educational tool, helping developers and operators understand the system's architecture, operational parameters, and data flow. Key abstractions include the concept of a trail (a session-based record), the role of PPR in similarity scoring, and the lifecycle of entries via pinning, visibility decay, and eviction. The presence of specific numerical values (e.g., damping factor of 0.85, cosine floors of 0.65 or 0.75) indicates that the module captures precise, tunable behaviors of the system.

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:README.md (unknown).

Cortex is a local knowledge substrate for AI coding agents that persists observations, decisions, and discoveries as an append-only datom log and projects them into a graph and vector store. It enables AI agents to recall, reflect on, and build upon prior work across sessions. The system operates through a write pipeline that validates, seals, and appends entries to a durable log before applying them to Neo4j (graph store) and Weaviate (vector store). Key features include episodic capture via `cortex observe`, retrieval using HippoRAG and ACT-R path-based algorithms, trail creation for grouping related work sessions, and self-healing through log replay. The architecture enforces data integrity with the datom log as the authoritative source of truth, and supports time-travel queries via historical transaction IDs. Configuration controls retrieval parameters like Personalized PageRank damping (0.85), seed top-k (10), activation weights, and visibility thresholds (0.05), while managing Ollama model usage with concurrency limits (2) and context size (32768).

This module provides a template for generating a narrative summary of a work trail. It defines a simple prompt template that instructs a summarization agent to produce a short narrative paragraph based on trail entries presented within delimiters. Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:README.md (unknown).

Cortex is a local knowledge substrate for AI coding agents that persists observations, decisions, and discoveries as an append-only datom log and projects them into a graph and vector store. It enables AI agents to recall, reflect on, and build upon prior work across sessions. The system operates through a write pipeline that validates, seals, and appends entries to a durable log before applying them to Neo4j (graph store) and Weaviate (vector store). Key features include episodic capture via `cortex observe`, retrieval using HippoRAG and ACT-R path-based algorithms, trail creation for grouping related work sessions, and self-healing through log replay. The architecture enforces data integrity with the datom log as the authoritative source of truth, and supports time-travel queries via historical transaction IDs. Configuration controls retrieval parameters like Personalized PageRank damping (0.85), seed top-k (10), activation weights, and visibility thresholds (0.05), while managing Ollama model usage with concurrency limits (2) and context size (32768).

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module unknown:per-file:README.md (unknown).

Cortex is a local knowledge substrate for AI coding agents that persists observations, decisions, and discoveries as an append-only datom log and projects them into a graph and vector store. It enables AI agents to recall, reflect on, and build upon prior work across sessions. The system operates through a write pipeline that validates, seals, and appends entries to a durable log before applying them to Neo4j (graph store) and Weaviate (vector store). Key features include episodic capture via `cortex observe`, retrieval using HippoRAG and ACT-R path-based algorithms, trail creation for grouping related work sessions, and self-healing through log replay. The architecture enforces data integrity with the datom log as the authoritative source of truth, and supports time-travel queries via historical transaction IDs. Configuration controls retrieval parameters like Personalized PageRank damping (0.85), seed top-k (10), activation weights, and visibility thresholds (0.05), while managing Ollama model usage with concurrency limits (2) and context size (32768).

Module unknown:per-file:docs/spec/cortex-spec.md (unknown).

This module defines the core architecture and operational behavior of a knowledge system called Cortex, focusing on a durable, append-only datom log as the authoritative source of truth for all persisted facts. The system ensures data integrity through a segmented log structure with checksums, transactional ordering via ULID, and self-healing recovery mechanisms that replay missing datoms on startup. It supports a comprehensive set of user-facing commands for episodic observation capture (e.g., `observe`), trail lifecycle management (e.g., `trail begin/end`), retrieval with multiple modes (e.g., default recall, traverse, path), and cross-project pattern analysis. Key abstractions include the datom log as a causal substrate, episodic and semantic frames for structured knowledge, and a layered retrieval pipeline combining vector similarity, Personalized PageRank (PPR), and ACT-R activation scoring. The system enforces operational health through a detailed CLI interface with commands for status, doctoring, and benchmarking, and implements principled forgetting via activation decay and explicit control (pin, unpin, evict). It also includes a migration path from a prior system (MemPalace) and ensures security through secret detection and file permission enforcement. The entire system is designed to be idempotent, resilient to partial failures, and reproducible through deterministic log replay, with all derived state being rebuildable from the log without requiring persistent storage of intermediate states.

Module go:per-package:internal/datom (go).

This module defines a canonical, immutable fact structure called a 'datom' that serves as the atomic unit in an append-only log. Each datom represents a (entity, attribute, value, transaction, operation) tuple with provenance metadata and a cryptographic checksum. The module ensures data integrity through a deterministic JSON serialization order and a SHA-256 checksum computed over the fields excluding the checksum itself. The checksum is updated via Seal and verified via Verify, detecting any tampering. The datom structure uses json.RawMessage to preserve raw bytes of attribute values, enabling precise round-trip serialization. A registry tracks attribute metadata, including whether they use last-write-wins (LWW) semantics for replay, which affects how values are resolved during log replay without altering write semantics. The module supports idempotent operations and provides a stable, canonical wire format for datoms in JSONL lines with a newline terminator.
